name: Metabase MCP Server
what_it_does: Metabase MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with Metabase's business intelligence platform — querying cards and dashboards, executing questions, exploring database metadata, and accessing analytics data from Metabase's self-hosted or cloud instances.
use_cases: Running Metabase queries from analytics reporting agents
Fetching dashboard data for business intelligence agents
Exploring database schema through Metabase's metadata from data agents
Generating ad-hoc analytics reports from question-answering agents
Embedding Metabase analytics in agent-driven workflows
Monitoring key business metrics from automated reporting agents
not_for: Teams using Looker, Tableau, or Power BI as their BI tool
best_when: An agent needs to query business intelligence data from a Metabase instance — especially for self-hosted BI environments.
avoid_when: You're using Looker, Tableau, Power BI, or another BI platform.

auth.notes: Metabase session tokens from username/password login. API keys available in newer versions (0.46+). No scope granularity.

pricing.notes: Metabase Open Source is free and self-hosted. Metabase Cloud and Pro add enterprise features. MCP server is community open source.

known_agent_gotchas: Session tokens expire — agents must handle re-authentication
Question/card IDs must be known before querying — discovery step needed
API key support added in Metabase 0.46 — older versions use session auth only
Database IDs are instance-specific — not transferable between Metabase instances
Query results can be large — agents should limit result sets
Metabase API is not officially versioned — may change between releases
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS configurable (self-hosted). Session auth via username/password. No scope granularity. SOC 2, GDPR for Cloud. Self-hosted security is operator responsibility.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS configurable. Session auth. No scope granularity. Self-hosted security is operator responsibility.
